description: Simulate two near-simultaneous settings saves; final .forge.json must be valid JSON with no partial content, reflecting last-write-wins atomically with no corrupted file.

execute.cmd: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),p=require('path'); const fp=p.join(process.cwd(),'.forge.json'); const cfgA={preset:'default',forgeMaster:{observer:{enabled:true,maxUsdPerDay:2}}}; const cfgB={preset:'default',forgeMaster:{observer:{enabled:false,maxUsdPerDay:3}}}; fs.writeFileSync(fp,JSON.stringify(cfgA,null,2)); fs.writeFileSync(fp,JSON.stringify(cfgB,null,2)); let final; try{final=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(fp,'utf-8'));}catch(e){throw new Error('final .forge.json is invalid JSON: '+e.message);} if(typeof final!=='object'||final===null)throw new Error('invalid object after concurrent save'); if(final.forgeMaster.observer.maxUsdPerDay!==3)throw new Error('last-write-wins failed: expected 3, got '+final.forgeMaster.observer.maxUsdPerDay); console.log('ok concurrent-save: last-write-wins, .forge.json is valid JSON');"
